codeReview.summary: The fix closes the prior scrutiny blockers: `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update.rs` now performs model/dimension validation in the shared core update path before reopening the vector store, so CLI and MCP both use the stored metadata dimension; `crates/vera-core/src/retrieval/search_service.rs` now treats any explicit-local provider initialization failure as fatal instead of silently degrading to BM25; and `crates/vera-core/src/local_models.rs` now asserts the real `.with_extension("part")` temp-file path in the cleanup regression test. I did not find a remaining functional blocker, but the shared update-path fix still lacks a focused regression test in `update_tests.rs`.

issues.description: The core metadata-dimension fix is implemented here, but `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update_tests.rs` still has no targeted regression case covering the new metadata-driven mismatch path (`embedding_dim` / `matching provider` searches return no hits). That leaves the MCP/shared update behavior added by this fix protected only by broad suite coverage rather than a focused regression test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill still does not fit small shared-core fixes that change MCP-relevant behavior: the procedure requires explicit MCP verification, but this handoff marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` after running only test/clippy/fmt.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:104-105` requires starting and testing MCP when a feature involves it. `/home/lamim/.factory/missions/f17cfc12-1e02-4226-b7e0-40720f4f4993/handoffs/2026-03-21T02-40-40-617Z__local-inference-fixes-r2__ec6c6280-3af7-428f-961a-89665fcd5a68.json:16-39` records only `cargo test --features local`, `cargo clippy --features local -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --all`, while this fix changes the shared `crates/vera-core/src/indexing/update.rs::update_repository` path used by MCP (`.factory/library/architecture.md:168-169`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library documents BM25 fallback for the shared search service, but it still does not capture the important exception that explicit local mode must fail instead of silently falling back when provider initialization fails.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:163-164` says the shared search service handles BM25 fallback, while `crates/vera-core/src/retrieval/search_service.rs:35-43` now bails immediately for `is_local` before the BM25 fallback path.

summary: Pass: commit `9d8d02e615b18b071fdb4654e263d2325a0bfb04` resolves the two prior blocking scrutiny findings and the local-model cleanup-test path nit for local inference. The only follow-up I found is non-blocking: the shared update-path fix would be safer with a focused regression test, and I noted two shared-state gaps around MCP verification guidance and documenting the explicit-local search fallback exception.
